prompt:
We grep JSON log dumps with ad-hoc scripts and it's misery. I want a tiny query language over arrays of plain log records (objects), pure TypeScript on node, no deps, node --test. Queries look like:

  level = "error" AND (status >= 500 OR msg ~ "timeout") AND NOT service = "billing"
  status >= 200 | stats count by service
  level = "error" | stats avg(duration) by service

Two public modules:

parse.ts — export parse(text) (whatever AST shape you like) and export class ParseError extends Error with a numeric .position — 1-based character position — and 'position N' appearing in the message. Point at the token that broke things; at end of input use one past the last character; an unterminated string points at its OPENING quote and mentions the string. Lexical rules: fields are [A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]* identifiers; values are double-quoted strings (\" and \\ escapes), numbers (negative, fractional ok), or the bare literals true/false; AND OR NOT are UPPERCASE keywords — a lowercase 'and' is just a stray identifier and should die as trailing junk. Comparisons: = != > >= < <= ~. Precedence NOT over AND over OR, parentheses group. Optionally one pipe: | stats count, | stats count by field, or | stats fn(field) [by field] with fn in sum avg min max — anything else after the pipe should say stats is expected, an unknown fn is named in the error.

engine.ts — export run(text, records). For a plain filter query: return THE SAME record objects that match, input order preserved. Matching rules matter: a record missing the field fails EVERY comparison on it, != included (so NOT duration > 0 finds records with no duration, but duration != 5 never does); = and != are strict, no type coercion ever ('status = "500"' matches nothing when status is numeric); > >= < <= only apply between two numbers, anything else is false; ~ is case-insensitive substring on strings, false otherwise. For a stats query: run the filter, then aggregate. Without by: [{ value }]. With by: one { group, value } row per distinct group value among matching records (records missing the group field are dropped), rows sorted ascending by String(group). count counts records; sum/avg/min/max fold the field's NUMERIC values only, ignoring records where it's missing or not a number — sum of nothing is 0, avg/min/max of nothing is null (a group can still appear with value null if all its values were junk).

test_parse.ts, test_filter.ts and test_stats.ts are the acceptance contract (node --test test_parse.ts test_filter.ts test_stats.ts) — build to them, don't edit them.
